# Bora Bora city guide

## Overview

Bora Bora is the postcard that's actually real. A volcanic island surrounded by a turquoise lagoon so vivid it looks photoshopped. Overwater bungalows, coral gardens, manta rays gliding beneath your feet, and some of the most spectacular sunsets on earth. Combined with nearby Moorea and Tahiti, French Polynesia is paradise found.

**Best time to visit**: May-Oct (dry season) for the best weather. Nov-Apr is warmer and wetter but has fewer tourists and lower prices.
**Currency**: CFP Franc (XPF). 110 XPF ≈ 1 USD. Credit cards accepted at resorts, cash needed at local spots.
**Language tip**: French and Tahitian are spoken. English is common at resorts. "Ia orana" (hello in Tahitian) and "Mauruuru" (thank you) bring big smiles.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Vaitape | Town center, local | Shopping, restaurants, post office, banks | Boat shuttle from resorts |
| Matira Beach | Iconic, public | The best public beach on the island, sunset views | Walk or bike from Vaitape |
| Motu Piti Aau | Luxury resort | Four Seasons, overwater bungalows, privacy | Resort boat shuttle |
| Motu Toopua | Resort, scenic | Hilton, panoramic views of Mount Otemanu | Resort boat shuttle |
| Faanui | Quiet, local | Ancient marae temples, local village life | Bike or car |
| Anau | Eastern shore | Coral gardens, snorkeling, Manta Ray point | Bike or car |
| Moorea (day trip) | Lush, mountainous | Hiking, pineapple plantations, snorkeling | Flight from Tahiti (10 min) or ferry |
| Tahiti/Papeete | Urban, cultural | Roulottes (food trucks), market, gateway hub | International flights land here |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Bloody Mary's | Seafood/grill | Vaitape | $$-$$$ | The famous sand-floor restaurant. Fresh fish displayed on ice, you pick your catch. Celebrity name plaques line the entrance. | Mahi mahi, lobster, cocktails with sandy toes |
| Aloe Cafe | Cafe/brunch | Vaitape | $$ | Small local cafe with fresh juices, crepes, and Polynesian dishes. A nice break from resort dining. | Poisson cru, fresh fruit crepe, tropical smoothie |
| Roulotte Matira | Food truck | Matira Beach | $ | Local food truck near the beach. Grilled fish, crepes, and panini at non-resort prices. Cash only. | Poisson cru, grilled fish plate |
| St. James Restaurant | French/Polynesian | Vaitape | $$$ | Upscale waterfront dining with French-Polynesian fusion. The tuna tartare with vanilla is exceptional. | Tuna tartare with vanilla, lobster |
| Lagoon Restaurant by Jean-Georges | Fine dining | Motu Toopua (St. Regis) | $$$ | Overwater restaurant by Jean-Georges Vongerichten. Dinner literally over the lagoon. Once-in-a-lifetime setting. | Tasting menu, poisson cru, champagne |
| Fare Manuia | Polynesian | Vaitape | $$ | Local Polynesian cuisine in a relaxed garden setting. One of the better non-resort dining options. | Poisson cru, grilled mahi mahi, coconut dessert |
| Maikai Marina and Yacht Club | Bar/restaurant | Vaitape | $$ | Casual waterfront spot popular with sailors and locals. Good pizza, cold Hinano beer, sunset views. | Pizza, Hinano beer, sunset |
| Papeete Roulottes | Food trucks | Tahiti (Papeete) | $ | The famous food truck gathering in Papeete. Grilled steaks, crepes, Chinese food, poisson cru. A Tahiti institution. | Poisson cru, steak frites, crepes |
| Snack Matira | Beach food | Matira Beach | $ | Simple beachside snack bar. Sandwiches, drinks, and the only food right on the public beach. | Baguette sandwich, Hinano beer |
| Overwater Bungalow Breakfast | Resort dining | Multiple resorts | $$$ | Canoe breakfast delivered to your overwater bungalow. Fresh fruit, pastries, eggs, and coffee floating to your deck. | The full canoe breakfast experience |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lagoon Snorkeling Tour | Nature | Lagoon | $$ | Half day | Boat tour with stops at coral gardens, shark and ray feeding, and swimming in impossibly blue water. |
| Mount Otemanu | Views | Central | Free (view) | N/A | The extinct volcanic peak that dominates the island. You can't climb to the top, but it's the backdrop to everything. |
| Matira Beach | Beach | Matira | Free | 2-4 hours | The only public beach and arguably the most beautiful in the South Pacific. White sand, gentle lagoon, palm trees. |
| Shark and Ray Snorkel | Adventure | Lagoon | $$ | 2-3 hours | Swim with blacktip reef sharks and stingrays in shallow lagoon water. Guides ensure safety. Unforgettable. |
| Jet Ski Lagoon Tour | Adventure | Lagoon | $$$ | 2 hours | Circle the island by jet ski with stops for snorkeling. The most thrilling way to see the lagoon. |
| Coral Gardens | Nature | East side | $$ | 1-2 hours | Shallow coral reef teeming with tropical fish. Some of the best snorkeling in the South Pacific. |
| Marae Temples | Culture | Faanui | Free | 1 hour | Ancient Polynesian temple platforms. Quiet, spiritual, and a reminder of Bora Bora before the resorts. |
| Moorea Day Trip | Day trip | Moorea | $$$ | Full day | Fly or ferry to Moorea for hiking, snorkeling with rays, and pineapple tasting. More rugged than Bora Bora. |
| Sunset Cruise | Scenic | Lagoon | $$-$$$ | 2 hours | Champagne catamaran cruise as the sun sets behind Mount Otemanu. Peak romance. |
| Scuba Diving | Adventure | Outer reef | $$$ | Half day | Manta rays, reef sharks, Napoleon wrasse, and vibrant coral. Multiple dive operators on the island. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Bloody Mary's Bar | Bar | Vaitape | $$-$$$ | The most famous bar on the island. Sand floor, thatched roof, strong cocktails. Open until late by Bora Bora standards. |
| Resort Bars | Hotel bars | Multiple | $$$ | Each resort has its own bar. Four Seasons, Conrad, and St. Regis all offer overwater cocktail experiences. |
| Maikai Marina Bar | Bar | Vaitape | $$ | Casual marina bar where sailors and locals mix. Hinano beer and sunset views. |
| Tiki Bar at InterContinental | Bar | Motu Piti Aau | $$$ | Tiki-themed overwater bar with Mount Otemanu views. Classic cocktails in a stunning setting. |
| Bora Bora Yacht Club | Bar | Vaitape | $$ | Waterfront drinks with a local crowd. Less resort polish, more genuine island vibes. |

## Transit tips

- **No public transit**: Bora Bora has no bus system. Transit is by boat, bike, or car.
- **Resort shuttles**: Most resorts offer free boat shuttles to Vaitape village. Check your hotel's schedule.
- **Bike rental**: The island is small (32 km around). Biking is a great way to explore, but it's hilly in parts.
- **Car rental**: Available but barely necessary. One main road circles the island.
- **Water taxi**: For getting between motus (small islands) and the main island. Arrange through your resort.
- **Getting there**: Fly to Tahiti (Papeete, PPT) first, then a 50-minute flight to Bora Bora (BOB). Air Tahiti is the main carrier.
- **Pro tip**: Everything is expensive in Bora Bora. Budget $300-500/day for a comfortable experience. All-inclusive packages help.

## Useful Tahitian/French phrases

| English | Tahitian/French | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Ia orana / Bonjour | ee-ah oh-RAH-nah / bohn-ZHOOR |
| Thank you | Mauruuru / Merci | mah-roo-ROO-roo / mehr-SEE |
| How are you? | Maita'i? | my-TAH-ee |
| Beautiful | Nehenehe | neh-heh-NEH-heh |
| Cheers! | Manuia! / Sante! | mah-noo-EE-ah / sahn-TAY |
| Goodbye | Nana / Au revoir | NAH-nah / oh reh-VWAHR |
| Yes/No | E/Aita / Oui/Non | eh / AY-tah / wee / nohn |
| Delicious | Maita'i roa! | my-TAH-ee ROH-ah |
